author: ChaosFish
raw:
[quote]
[i]Originally written [here](http://www.chaosfish.hpg.ig.com.br/lba/howtocreate.html#text)[/i]
[b]I recommended you to use LBA Text Editor. If you have Windows XP, LBA Text Editor [i]will not work properly[/i] unless you will run it in compatibility mode. To do that: right click on "LBATxt.exe" (or it's shortcut) and click "Properties". Click on the "Compatibility" tab, and choose "Windows 95" compatibility mode.[/b]
[/quote]

Kakadudu's LBA Text should work under XP in this way, and it will probably go much smoother.
cooked:
Originally written here
I recommended you to use LBA Text Editor. If you have Windows XP, LBA Text Editor will not work properly unless you will run it in compatibility mode. To do that: right click on LBATxt.exe (or its shortcut) and click Properties. Click on the Compatibility tab, and choose Windows 95 compatibility mode.

Kakadudus LBA Text should work under XP in this way, and it will probably go much smoother.

author: Zink
raw:
Err, no. You are wrong. ONLY entries with EVEN indexes contain text (except blank ones ofcourse). Odd entries contain text indexes that are not usable and there is no need to edit them.

The file with error messages is the second one (text002.lbt) and you have to replace the second file with it. 

You probably did a mistake with unpacking, that's why this file has "text001.lbt" name.
cooked:
Err, no. You are wrong. ONLY entries with EVEN indexes contain text (except blank ones ofcourse). Odd entries contain text indexes that are not usable and there is no need to edit them.

The file with error messages is the second one (text002.lbt) and you have to replace the second file with it.

You probably did a mistake with unpacking, thats why this file has text001.lbt name.

topic_title: LBA Modifications: Quick Start & Modding Tools [updated: 01-04-2009]
author: ChaosFish
raw:
[b]What are mods?[/b]
Mods are modifications to the game's resources. Resources include scenes, models, animations, videos, etc. but also smaller things such as texts and fonts. A complete mod is a mod that uses the original game engine, but changes everything else.

[b]LBA mods[/b]
So you want to start modding LBA? Rock on!
At this point you can change almost everything, thanks to our very cool LBA tools developers. Check the list of tools in this thread to see what you can do.

Other than that, you can replace any entry type with any other entry from the game in the same type. (for example, replace Twinsen's model with Zo's model)

[b]LBA File Information[/b]
For comprehensive information about the entires of the LBA package files, see the [[b]LBA File Information[/b]](http://lba.fishos.net/fileinfo/) website.
The site also has a large variety of information about other technical aspects of the games, such as file format specifications.
You can also submit your information if it isn't already listed there.

[b]LBA Translations[/b]
[Little Bit Adventure](http://lba.fishos.net/bit/) is a site dedicated to LBA mods - currently only for translations and dubbing. There you'll find [sets](http://lba.fishos.net/bit/sets.html) of compiled text and font files that goes together, uncompiled LBA [fonts](http://lba.fishos.net/bit/fonts.html) and LBA [text](http://lba.fishos.net/bit/text.html) files, and [guides](http://lba.fishos.net/bit/guides.html) for telling you how to use them in the game and how to make your own translation and dubbing.

[b]Tools[/b]
Here are some extremely useful programs that will help you to make your mods.
[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=393]Yaz0r[/drupal] - [yaz0r's blog](http://www.yaz0r.net/)[/b][/u]
[TwinEngine](http://prdownloads.sourceforge.net/twin-e/twin-e-win32-0.1.zip?download): [i](official beta)[/i] LBA1 recode in open source made by reverse engineering. You must also download [SDL.dll](http://www.libsdl.org/release/SDL-1.2.13-win32.zip) and extract to same directory for it to work. ([get current source code](http://twin-e.svn.sourceforge.net/viewvc/twin-e/))
[drupal=disknode/get/2660/lbaModelViewer.zip]LBA Model Viewer[/drupal]: View all LBA1 & LBA2 models and their animations, from any angle you'd like.
[drupal=disknode/get/2661/lba2ExtViewer.zip]LBA2 Island Viewer[/drupal]: View all LBA2 outside maps, from any angle and position you'd like. Still quite buggy.

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=3728]david38[/drupal][/b][/u]
[LBA Perspective Map Viewer](http://fishos.net/stuff/mbn/david38/PerspectiveMapViewerV3.rar): View all LBA1 & LBA2 isometric scenes as real 3D scenes from any angle and position you'd like.

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=234]Zink[/drupal] - [Emerald Moon Base](http://lba.kazekr.net/)[/b][/u]
[LBA Package Editor](http://lba.kazekr.net/d.php?n=LBAPackEditor.zip): Professional LBA compiler and de-compiler. This program can extract entries of LBA package files, and replace any entry with your own file. One of the main improvements over LBADeCOMP is the ability to extract hidden entries.
[LBArchitect](http://lba.kazekr.net/d.php?n=LBArchitect.zip): View and edit rooms from LBA1&2 (and islands from LBA1). You can now create your own islands.
[LBA Font Editor](http://lba.kazekr.net/d.php?n=LBAFontEditor.zip): Edit the LBA font file. Includes clipboard support.
[LBA Text Editor 2](http://lba.kazekr.net/d.php?n=LBATextEditor2.zip): Edit the LBA text files, in any language (character mapping feature). Fancy interface.
[LBA Shape Editor](http://lba.kazekr.net/d.php?n=LBAShapeEditor.zip): Edit LBA2 shapes (stars flying out of characters when they get hurt, etc.).
[LBA HQR manager](http://lba.kazekr.net/d.php?n=LBAHqr.zip): Replace the entries inside HQR archives with your own files.

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=588]alexfont[/drupal] - [The Sacred Carrot](http://sacredcarrot.xesf.net/)[/b][/u]
[LBAnimator Studio](http://sacredcarrot.xesf.net/downloads/programs/LBAnimatorStudio.rar): Edit and view all LBA1 3D models and animations in a real 3D environment made in OpenGL. 
[LBA Story Coder](http://sacredcarrot.xesf.net/downloads/programs/LBAStoryCoder.rar): Edit the LBA1 and LBA2 scenes' dynamic objects (doors, character, etc.). This program also works with LBArchitect to display the dynamic objects.
[LBA Screen Viewer](http://sacredcarrot.xesf.net/downloads/programs/LBAScreenViewer.rar): View all LBA special images, including LBA1 movies. You can also create LBA movies and images.
[LBA 3D Object Viewer](http://sacredcarrot.xesf.net/downloads/programs/LBA3DObjectViewer.rar): View all LBA1 3D models. Drag an LBA1 model file to the program to use, or define as lm1 tool in LBA Package Editor.
[LBA Holomap Locations](http://sacredcarrot.xesf.net/downloads/programs/LBAHolomapLocations.rar): Edit LBA1 holomap arrow information (entry 9 in RESS.HQR).

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=1288]OBrasilo[/drupal] - [Little Big AREA](http://www.islandcx.net/)[/b][/u]
[LBA Resource Manager](http://www.islandcx.net/RManager.zip): Professional HQR file manager. Contains advanced features: scripts, Hex viewing, compression of entries, and more. You can also create your own HQR's from scratch. You need to have [comct332.ocx](http://www.afreeocx.com/ocx/download/2875/comct332.ocx) in your Windows System32 folder for it to work.
[LBA Model Manager](http://www.islandcx.net/MManager.zip): Edit LBA1 models by changing their numeric values, and save them into the game.
[LBA Text File Editor](http://www.islandcx.net/textedit.zip): Edit the LBA text, in any language (character mapping feature). Instant in-game preview while typing. Also download the [update](http://www.islandcx.net/Textexe.zip).
[LBA Scene Manager](http://www.islandcx.net/SManager.zip): Edit the LBA1 and LBA2 scenes' dynamic objects.
[LBA2 Saved Game Editor](http://www.islandcx.net/LBA2SGE.zip): Edit LBA2 savegames with a user friendly interface.
[LBA File 3D Editor](http://www.islandcx.net/EditorFile3D.zip): Edit the LBA1 & LBA2 character information file (info of which models to use for which characters).
[LBA Brick Layout Library Editor](http://www.islandcx.net/BLLEdit.zip): Edit the scenes' stock of of isometric bricks in both LBA1 and LBA2.
[LBA2 Island Polygon Viewer](http://www.islandcx.net/ILEView.zip): View the polygon surface of LBA2 outside scenes.
[LBA2 Credits Manager](http://www.islandcx.net/LBA2Edit.zip): Edit the credits file in LBA2, including the credits and the credits scene.
[LBA/Relentless Area/Coordinate Hacker](http://www.islandcx.net/Lbahack.zip): Edit position, area code and magic level in LBA1 savegames.
[MS-DOS Font Editor](http://www.islandcx.net/FontEdit.zip): Edit the MS-DOS font LBA2 uses for small sized text (used on the keyboard config menu).
[LBA2 Font Replacer](http://www.islandcx.net/LBA2Replace.zip): Replace the MS-DOS font stored in LBA2 executable file with any other MS-DOS .FNT file.
[LBA Font File Converter](http://www.islandcx.net/Fontconv.zip): Convert LBA fonts into .FON font format.
[WinHQR](http://www.islandcx.net/winhqr.zip): An older less advanced version of LBA Resource Manager. You can extract without decompression, replace any sort of entry, and more.

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=243]kargeor (George)[/drupal] - [LBA Projects opensource](http://lbaprojects.sourceforge.net/)[/b][/u]
[LBA Font](http://prdownloads.sourceforge.net/lbaprojects/LBAFont_275_exe.zip?download): Edit the LBA font. Includes import from standard font files support.  (also developed by [b]OBrasilo[/b])
[LBA Text](http://prdownloads.sourceforge.net/lbaprojects/LBAText_200_exe.zip?download): Edit the LBA text, in any language (character mapping feature). Simple interface.

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=1]El Muerte[/drupal] - [Magicball Network](http://magicball.net/)[/b][/u]
[drupal=disknode/get/1663/lbadecomp12214.exe]LBADeCOMP[/drupal]: The first big program. It allows you to extract resources from LBA1 and LBA2. It also includes [u]LBA Text Viewer[/u], [u]LBA Image Viewer[/u], [u]LBA Packer[/u] (for compiling a fresh LBA archive), and detailed help files. You must also download and install the [drupal=disknode/get/1662/lbadepak.exe]LBADeCOMP library package[/drupal] for it to work. It has a bug: it may hang when trying to extract files to hard disk. If that happens you can just extract the files to a floppy.
[drupal=disknode/get/1652/lba-sge.exe]LBA Savegame Editor[/drupal]: Edit LBA1 and LBA2 savegames. You can edit the LBA1 location, inventory, position and character status. In LBA2 you can only change the location. This program has some problems with newer machines.
[drupal=disknode/get/1653/LBASGE2.1.0pre4-setup.exe]LBA1 Savegame Editor 2[/drupal]: A newer version of the LBA1 savegame editor. You can now also change the holomap arrows, the completed quests, and the viewed movies.

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=1232]LBAWinOwns[/drupal][/b][/u]
[LBA Image Creator](http://miffoljud.googlepages.com/LBAImageCreator1.1.zip): Convert any image into an LBA image format. (most kinds of LBA images are supported)

[u][b][drupal=forum/member.php?u=3062]K.A.T.[/drupal][/b][/u]
[LBA LIM Editor](http://toya.net.pl/~kat15/LBALIMEditor.zip): Editor for *.lim/*.raw files. You can also load and save into other known image formats.


[b]This Forum[/b]
This forum is for any discussion about LBA modifications. If you start any modding project, even a small one, you are more than welcome to open a new thread about it. If you seek help, please check out the [LBA File Information](http://lba.fishos.net/fileinfo/) website and [Little Bit Adventure](http://lba.fishos.net/bit/)'s guides before asking questions.
Note that this forum is only for discussions about mods to the LBA games!

LBA Screen Viewer: View all LBA special images, including LBA1 movies. You can also create LBA movies and images.
LBA 3D Object Viewer: View all LBA1 3D models. Drag an LBA1 model file to the program to use, or define as lm1 tool in LBA Package Editor.
LBA Holomap Locations: Edit LBA1 holomap arrow information (entry 9 in RESS.HQR).

[drupal=forum/member.php?u=1288]OBrasilo[/drupal] - Little Big AREA
LBA Resource Manager: Professional HQR file manager. Contains advanced features: scripts, Hex viewing, compression of entries, and more. You can also create your own HQRs from scratch. You need to have comct332.ocx in your Windows System32 folder for it to work.
LBA Model Manager: Edit LBA1 models by changing their numeric values, and save them into the game.
LBA Text File Editor: Edit the LBA text, in any language (character mapping feature). Instant in-game preview while typing. Also download the update.
LBA Scene Manager: Edit the LBA1 and LBA2 scenes dynamic objects.
LBA2 Saved Game Editor: Edit LBA2 savegames with a user friendly interface.
LBA File 3D Editor: Edit the LBA1 & LBA2 character information file (info of which models to use for which characters).
LBA Brick Layout Library Editor: Edit the scenes stock of of isometric bricks in both LBA1 and LBA2.
LBA2 Island Polygon Viewer: View the polygon surface of LBA2 outside scenes.
LBA2 Credits Manager: Edit the credits file in LBA2, including the credits and the credits scene.
LBA/Relentless Area/Coordinate Hacker: Edit position, area code and magic level in LBA1 savegames.
MS-DOS Font Editor: Edit the MS-DOS font LBA2 uses for small sized text (used on the keyboard config menu).
LBA2 Font Replacer: Replace the MS-DOS font stored in LBA2 executable file with any other MS-DOS .FNT file.
LBA Font File Converter: Convert LBA fonts into .FON font format.
WinHQR: An older less advanced version of LBA Resource Manager. You can extract without decompression, replace any sort of entry, and more.

[drupal=forum/member.php?u=243]kargeor (George)[/drupal] - LBA Projects opensource
LBA Font: Edit the LBA font. Includes import from standard font files support. (also developed by OBrasilo)
LBA Text: Edit the LBA text, in any language (character mapping feature). Simple interface.

[drupal=forum/member.php?u=1]El Muerte[/drupal] - Magicball Network
[drupal=disknode/get/1663/lbadecomp12214.exe]LBADeCOMP[/drupal]: The first big program. It allows you to extract resources from LBA1 and LBA2. It also includes LBA Text Viewer, LBA Image Viewer, LBA Packer (for compiling a fresh LBA archive), and detailed help files. You must also download and install the [drupal=disknode/get/1662/lbadepak.exe]LBADeCOMP library package[/drupal] for it to work. It has a bug: it may hang when trying to extract files to hard disk. If that happens you can just extract the files to a floppy.
[drupal=disknode/get/1652/lba-sge.exe]LBA Savegame Editor[/drupal]: Edit LBA1 and LBA2 savegames. You can edit the LBA1 location, inventory, position and character status. In LBA2 you can only change the location. This program has some problems with newer machines.
[drupal=disknode/get/1653/LBASGE2.1.0pre4-setup.exe]LBA1 Savegame Editor 2[/drupal]: A newer version of the LBA1 savegame editor. You can now also change the holomap arrows, the completed quests, and the viewed movies.

[drupal=forum/member.php?u=1232]LBAWinOwns[/drupal]
LBA Image Creator: Convert any image into an LBA image format. (most kinds of LBA images are supported)

[drupal=forum/member.php?u=3062]K.A.T.[/drupal]
LBA LIM Editor: Editor for .lim/.raw files. You can also load and save into other known image formats.

This Forum
This forum is for any discussion about LBA modifications. If you start any modding project, even a small one, you are more than welcome to open a new thread about it. If you seek help, please check out the LBA File Information website and Little Bit Adventures guides before asking questions.
Note that this forum is only for discussions about mods to the LBA games!

author: ChaosFish
raw:
([Latest LBA File Information](/t/latest-lba-file-information-27th-november-2004/6708/1))

If you have new information for the LBA File Information project, please post it in this thread.

[b]Use [LBA Package Editor with file info](http://80.55.102.190/~zink/lba/d.php?n=LBAPackEditor.zip) (not LBADeCOMP), and choose "Display all entires" and "Index all entries". Then start making the list.[/b]


[b]Template submission for packages entries listing[/b]
[quote]
[b]FILENAME[/b]

%1 001 ~1 Twinsen running *
%2 002 ~1 Twinsen fishing *
%4 003 ~2 Twinsun model *
%1 004 ~1 Zo chatting with Sendell *

\-END-

~1 = animation
~2 = model
[/quote]

In the beggining of each line, place %+(number) (one spacing after the number).
%1 stands for normal entry
%2 stands for hidden entry
%3 stands for repeated entry
%4 stands for blank entry

The number of the entry should be 3 digits. Between the number of the entry and the description there should be ~+(number) (one spacing before the ~ and one spacing after the number).
Make one number for each entry type, and then (like in the template) explain which is each.

In the description, make capital letters only in the start of a sentence and in the first letter of a name. Make the descriptions as short as possible.

In the end, place * (one spacing before the *).


I'm asking you to use this template just for I won't have to do it myself. (it takes a lot of time)
\-----------

If you any have information you can give about LBA files other than packages entries listing, please start a new thread about it in the [LBA Modifications](https://forum.magicball.net/forumdisplay.php?f=37) forum.

I just can describe the firsts 147 entries.
The other entries are probably small object's designs that apeear in the roons.

author: Zink
raw:
I have discovered one file inside ress.hqr (I know this isn't much, but...):

RESS.HQR from LBA 2

%1 047 ~1 Shapes (stars, hearts, etc.) *

~1 = Shapes file

This file contains these small stars that fly off Twinsen when it gets hurt, it also contains other shapes, such as hearts when Zoe kisses Twinsen, and other shapes. I made a little and quick-made viewer, if you want to see them all:

http://80.55.102.190/~zink/other/LBAShape.rar [168kB]

I think I could write the specification of it and/or make an editor if someone would make use of it.

author: Zink
raw:
LBA 2 
[b]lba_bkg.hqr[/b]

The entries with following numbers are layout libraries. They contain groups of bricks that are then used to design rooms in Lba2. Each entry contains brick groups (these groups can be called objects, but it's not precise definition) specific to room appearance. The meaning of descriptions below is "what groups from each entry are used for".

%1 180 ~1 Houses on Citadel Island *
%1 181 ~1 Sewers *
%1 182 ~1 Cellars and some buildings (gray bricks) *
%1 183 ~1 Caves (e.g. temple of Bu) *
%1 184 ~1 Buildings on Emerald Moon *
%1 185 ~1 Buildings on Desert Island *
%1 186 ~1 Esmer shuttles *
%1 187 ~1 Factories and buildings on Zeelish *
%1 188 ~1 Imperial hotel *
%1 189 ~1 Rooms in Emperor's palace *
%1 190 ~1 Dark Monk's statue *
%1 191 ~1 Buildings in the under-gas *
%1 192 ~1 Mosquibees buildings *
%1 193 ~1 Casino, bar, and souvenir shop *
%1 194 ~1 Gazogem rafinery *
%1 195 ~1 Buildings on Francos island *
%1 196 ~1 Shuttle control rooms *
%1 197 ~1 Sewers and other rooms on Island CX *

Entries from ~198 to ~18100 are bricks, not necessary to describe them all I think.

~1 Brick Layout Libraries

Viewer for both bricks and libraries (and for whole rooms also) will come in a couple of days.

author: Battler
raw:
Fix for the LBA 2 SCENE.HQR file:
 
 First entry = counter, made of 4 bytes. 1st byte = total count of scenes, 2nd byte = cound of outside scenes. ;)
cooked:
Fix for the LBA 2 SCENE.HQR file:

First entry = counter, made of 4 bytes. 1st byte = total count of scenes, 2nd byte = cound of outside scenes.

author: Battler
raw:
NOTE: LBA 1J = Japanese LBA 1


LBA 1J:
This file contains the LBAJ texts and their indexes.
 
[b]TEXT.HQR[/b]
         
%1 001 ~ Text index for 002
%2 002 ~ Japanese: Options and menus
%1 003 ~ Text index for 004
%2 004 ~ Japanese: Credits
%1 005 ~ Text index for 006
%2 006 ~ Japanese: Inventory, stories and Holomap locations
%1 007 ~ Text index for 008
 %2 008 ~ Japanese: Citadel Island
%1 009 ~ Text index for 010
  %2 010 ~ Japanese: Principal Island
%1 011 ~ Text index for 012
  %2 012 ~ Japanese: White Leaf Desert
%1 013 ~ Text index for 014
  %2 014 ~ Japanese: Proxima Island
%1 015 ~ Text index for 016
  %2 016 ~ Japanese: Rebellion Island
%1 017 ~ Text index for 018
  %2 018 ~ Japanese: Hamalayi mountains - southern range
%1 019 ~ Text index for 020
   %2 020 ~ Japanese: Hamalayi mountains - northern range
%1 021 ~ Text index for 022
    %2 022 ~ Japanese: Tippett Island
%1 023 ~ Text index for 024
     %2 024 ~ Japanese: Brundle Island
%1 025 ~ Text index for 026
     %2 026 ~ Japanese: Fortress Island
%1 027 ~ Text index for 028
     %2 028 ~ Japanese: Polar Island

\-END- 

~1 = text index table
~2 = text table


BTW, there's an error in the LBA File Information thread - it still links to http://chaosfish.free.fr .
And there's also an error on the LBA File Information site - it incorrectly names the LBA 2 file SPRIRAW.HQR as SPRITERAW.HQR.

Edit: I have one question:
\- ChaosFish: Is it alright for you that I make an up-to-date LBA File Information site on http://www.obrasilero.com/lbafileinfo? Since I could do it with a nice look and in PHP code.
So, what do you think? ;)

author: Zink
raw:
Sorry, I've not been here for some time...

Yes, I am working on it more or less constantly, and almost everything is done. There are still some things, that absolutely must be done before the release:
 - submission forms and system (started doing it yesterday)
 - editor for HQR entries (forgot to convert it from text file version to database)
 - account system for admins and editors.

I estimate the time to release for about two months.

Yes, it will not be hard to add more game versions to the engine. With copying the engine, there is one thing, that the tables in the database will have to be created manually (but I may write an install script for that, if it's necessary).
cooked:
Sorry, Ive not been here for some time

Yes, I am working on it more or less constantly, and almost everything is done. There are still some things, that absolutely must be done before the release:

  • submission forms and system (started doing it yesterday)
  • editor for HQR entries (forgot to convert it from text file version to database)
  • account system for admins and editors.

I estimate the time to release for about two months.

Yes, it will not be hard to add more game versions to the engine. With copying the engine, there is one thing, that the tables in the database will have to be created manually (but I may write an install script for that, if its necessary).

author: xesf

You don't need to make an installation file, just make a TXT file with the MySQL dump (only structure).

cooked:

author: Zink
raw: You mean: in the extension edit page? You can always type in the hex code. You couldn't do that if it was a combo, that's why I made it so.
cooked: You mean: in the extension edit page? You can always type in the hex code. You couldnt do that if it was a combo, thats why I made it so.

author: xesf
raw: Ah, I didn't know that, because I saw a color name like "lightcoral" (seams red) and didn't know what kind of colors like that were possible. So, you don't need to change anything ;). Colors in Hex values its perfect.
cooked: Ah, I didnt know that, because I saw a color name like lightcoral (seams red) and didnt know what kind of colors like that were possible. So, you dont need to change anything ;). Colors in Hex values its perfect.

author: Zink
raw:
All colours supported by html standard are possible. Here is the list: http://www.htmlgoodies.com/tutorials/colors/article.php/3478961

I didn't make any name-to-code conversion, the engine just puts the string you type in as the colour. You can do the same in all html pages, e.g. type "black" instead of #000000.
cooked:
All colours supported by html standard are possible. Here is the list: 10 Basic Hex Color Codes & Names | HTML Goodies

I didnt make any name-to-code conversion, the engine just puts the string you type in as the colour. You can do the same in all html pages, e.g. type black instead of #000000.

links.anchor_text: 10 Basic Hex Color Codes & Names | HTML Goodies

author: morshem
raw:
Is this project still alive? I was bored and extracted the voice files from LBA 2, and submitted information on the site. But I wasn't sure it will actually get anywhere, so I post it here too :p .

This is for en_gam.vox (LBA 2):


EN_GAM001
You just found your Holomap! To use it, look at the inventory by pressing <shift> or simply press <tab>.

EN_GAM002
You just found your Magic Ball! When you wear your ancestral Tunic you'll be able to bounce it at your enemies!

EN_GAM003
You have found three darts.

EN_GAM004
You just found Sendell's Ball. Now you have reached a new level of magic: Red Ball. It will also enable Sendell to contact you in case of danger.

EN_GAM005
You have just found your ancestral Tunic and Sendell's Medallion. By storing Magic, it will enable you to bounce your ball at your enemies.

EN_GAM006
You just found the Pearl of Incandescent. These Pearls are highly prized by magicians. They will enable you to complete a spell.

EN_GAM007
Your neighbor just gave you a pyramid shaped key.

EN_GAM008
Jerome Baldino just gave you a part that Zo needs to fix the car.

EN_GAM010
You have found a laser-pistol. It needs a rock crystal to work properly.

EN_GAM011
You just seized the emperor's sword. Now you can face the hussars guarding the Emperor's palace.

EN_GAM012
You just found a Wannie's Glove. Use it to repell the fireballs your enemies throw at you.

EN_GAM013
You just found the Proto-Pack. Put it on and you'll fly a few inches above the ground.

EN_GAM014
You have a ferry ticket.

EN_GAM015
You just acquired a superb Nitro-Meca-Penguin. Select it in the inventory and it'll explode a few seconds later.

EN_GAM016
You just found a can of Gazogem. This fuel will allow you to get your new super jet-pack.

EN_GAM017
You just found the Ring of Dissidents. It's their identification sign, and it will allow you to be identified by all the dissidents that you meet.

EN_GAM018
You just found a flask of gallic acid. It's often used to restore ancient documents.

EN_GAM019
You just learned the ferryman's song. It will help you to get to Mosquibee Island.

EN_GAM021
You just found the customer's umbrella

EN_GAM022
You just picked up a gem.

EN_GAM023
You own the Horn of the Blue Triton, you can use it to heal yourself or someone else.

EN_GAM024
You now have a blowgun. It fires darts fast and accurately.

EN_GAM025
You have found a itinary token. You can now use an Esmer shuttle.

EN_GAM026
You just found a slice of tart!

EN_GAM027
You now own a portable radio. You can now receive calls from your friends.

EN_GAM028
You just picked a Garden Balsam plant.

EN_GAM029
You now own a Magic Slate, which allows you to keep a copy of all kinds of images.

EN_GAM030
You have found a translator. Now you'll be able to understand the Esmer language.

EN_GAM031
You just received your wizard's diploma. Plus you just earned a new level of magic: Green Ball.

EN_GAM032
You now own the Francos key fragment.

EN_GAM033
You now own the Sups key fragment.

EN_GAM034
You now own the Mosquibees key fragment.

EN_GAM035
You now own the Wannies key fragment.

EN_GAM036
You just found the key of the passage which leads to island CX.

EN_GAM037
You just bought a pick-ax. Use it to dig or detach rocks and pieces of crystal.

EN_GAM038
You just found the Francos Burgermaster's key.

EN_GAM039
You just found the Francos Burgermaster's notes. They'll help you find the Francos fragment.

EN_GAM040
You now own a protective spell.

EN_GAM045
You now own the key to Dark Monk's temple. Plus you just reached your last level of magic: Fire Ball.

EN_GAM046
You just found a memory viewer.

EN_GAM047
You just found the blowtron. Use it to shoot faster and more powerfully.

EN_GAM048
You just found a parade outfit for the wizards. It'll make you look like an old wizard.

EN_GAM049
Jerome just gave you the super-jetpack. It will allow you to reach places you couldn't get to using the proto-pack.

EN_GAM050
You just found a piece of the Crystal of Celebration. Now you can reload your laser-pistol.

EN_GAM051
You now own a laser-pistol in working condition. It gives you enough firepower to reach distant targets that your magical ball cannot reach..

EN_GAM052
You just found the Ring of Lightning! Use it to find Sendell's Ball.

EN_GAM286
With my eyes focused on the center of the bazaar, my back is glued to a natural parasol where I'd love to take a nap, but I must keep a sportive mood.

EN_GAM287
It seems there are enough light posts and arches in the village to count the correct number of steps. Once there, the fences to my left point in the wrong direction. It's on the other side, down in the basin near the gas that my buried treasure lies.

%N 1 ~47 You just found your Holomap! To use it, look at the inventory by pressing <shift> or simply press <tab>.
%N 2 ~47 You just found your Magic Ball! When you wear your ancestral Tunic you'll be able to bounce it at your enemies!
%N 3 ~47 You have found three darts.
%N 4 ~47 You just found Sendell's Ball. Now you have reached a new level of magic: Red Ball. It will also enable Sendell to contact you in case of danger.
%N 5 ~47 You have just found your ancestral Tunic and Sendell's Medallion. By storing Magic, it will enable you to bounce your ball at your enemies.
%N 6 ~47 You just found the Pearl of Incandescent. These Pearls are highly prized by magicians. They will enable you to complete a spell.
%N 7 ~47 Your neighbor just gave you a pyramid shaped key.
%N 8 ~47 Jerome Baldino just gave you a part that Zo needs to fix the car.
%N 10 ~47 You have found a laser-pistol. It needs a rock crystal to work properly.
%N 11 ~47 You just seized the emperor's sword. Now you can face the hussars guarding the Emperor's palace.
%N 12 ~47 You just found a Wannie's Glove. Use it to repell the fireballs your enemies throw at you.
%N 13 ~47 You just found the Proto-Pack. Put it on and you'll fly a few inches above the ground.
%N 14 ~47 You have a ferry ticket.
%N 15 ~47 You just acquired a superb Nitro-Meca-Penguin. Select it in the inventory and it'll explode a few seconds later.
%N 16 ~47 You just found a can of Gazogem. This fuel will allow you to get your new super jet-pack.
%N 17 ~47 You just found the Ring of Dissidents. It's their identification sign, and it will allow you to be identified by all the dissidents that you meet.
%N 18 ~47 You just found a flask of gallic acid. It's often used to restore ancient documents.
%N 19 ~47 You just learned the ferryman's song. It will help you to get to Mosquibee Island.
%N 21 ~47 You just found the customer's umbrella
%N 22 ~47 You just picked up a gem.
%N 23 ~47 You own the Horn of the Blue Triton, you can use it to heal yourself or someone else.
%N 24 ~47 You now have a blowgun. It fires darts fast and accurately.
%N 25 ~47 You have found a itinary token. You can now use an Esmer shuttle.
%N 26 ~47 You just found a slice of tart!
%N 27 ~47 You now own a portable radio. You can now receive calls from your friends.
%N 28 ~47 You just picked a Garden Balsam plant.
%N 29 ~47 You now own a Magic Slate, which allows you to keep a copy of all kinds of images.
%N 30 ~47 You have found a translator. Now you'll be able to understand the Esmer language.
%N 31 ~47 You just received your wizard's diploma. Plus you just earned a new level of magic: Green Ball.
%N 32 ~47 You now own the Francos key fragment.
%N 33 ~47 You now own the Sups key fragment.
%N 34 ~47 You now own the Mosquibees key fragment.
%N 35 ~47 You now own the Wannies key fragment.
%N 36 ~47 You just found the key of the passage which leads to island CX.
%N 37 ~47 You just bought a pick-ax. Use it to dig or detach rocks and pieces of crystal.
%N 38 ~47 You just found the Francos Burgermaster's key.
%N 39 ~47 You just found the Francos Burgermaster's notes. They'll help you find the Francos fragment.
%N 40 ~47 You now own a protective spell.
%N 45 ~47 You now own the key to Dark Monk's temple. Plus you just reached your last level of magic: Fire Ball.
%N 46 ~47 You just found a memory viewer.
%N 47 ~47 You just found the blowtron. Use it to shoot faster and more powerfully.
%N 48 ~47 You just found a parade outfit for the wizards. It'll make you look like an old wizard.
%N 49 ~47 Jerome just gave you the super-jetpack. It will allow you to reach places you couldn't get to using the proto-pack.
%N 50 ~47 You just found a piece of the Crystal of Celebration. Now you can reload your laser-pistol.
%N 51 ~47 You now own a laser-pistol in working condition. It gives you enough firepower to reach distant targets that your magical ball cannot reach..
%N 52 ~47 You just found the Ring of Lightning! Use it to find Sendell's Ball.
%N 286 ~47 With my eyes focused on the center of the bazaar, my back is glued to a natural parasol where I'd love to take a nap, but I must keep a sportive mood.
%N 287 ~47 It seems there are enough light posts and arches in the village to count the correct number of steps. Once there, the fences to my left point in the wrong direction. It's on the other side, down in the basin near the gas that my buried treasure lies.
[/quote]

[b][Download LBA File Information](http://chaosfish.free.fr/mbn/LBAfileinfo.zip)[/b] - 'HTML Help' document[b]

[View LBA File Information online](http://chaosfish.free.fr/lbafileinfo/)[/b]

LBA File Information: The project is a document that contains LBA file information. The document currently only includes packages content, and that too is still mostly incomplete.
The goal of the project is to have all information possible about the LBA files.

Currently having:
* LBA1
    * HQR
        * ANIM.HQR

        * FILE3D.HQR
        * FLA_GIF.HQR

        * SAMPLES.HQR (needs rewrite)

author: ChaosFish
raw:
I converted Lenny's and Evyatar's voices to LBA1 format, and the sound quality is really ok, except for a little background hiss.

I couldn't get Adobe Audition to save it in 11025SampleRate+8Bit without hiss. Anyone has an idea what to do?
cooked:
I converted Lennys and Evyatars voices to LBA1 format, and the sound quality is really ok, except for a little background hiss.

I couldnt get Adobe Audition to save it in 11025SampleRate+8Bit without hiss. Anyone has an idea what to do?

author: ChaosFish
raw:
I tried "Steinberg WaveLab" and the sound quality turned out OK in LBA1's 11025SampleRate+8Bit and without a noticeable white noise.
The problem is, when I converted it to LBA1 voc file with Awave Audio, it gets to be just like before, with all the white noise.

But ok, I'll try Sound Forge.
cooked:
I tried Steinberg WaveLab and the sound quality turned out OK in LBA1s 11025SampleRate+8Bit and without a noticeable white noise.
The problem is, when I converted it to LBA1 voc file with Awave Audio, it gets to be just like before, with all the white noise.

But ok, Ill try Sound Forge.

author: ChaosFish
raw:
Sound Forge didn't help, but with a cooperation between WavLab, DeNoiser and [wav2voc](http://hem.spray.se/joakim.slettengren/files/wav2voc.zip), I managed to get a reasonable quality!

Here is Evyatar's voice in working LBA1 format (I hope you don't mind me posting it ET).

Alright quality right?

[TWINSEN.zip|attachment](upload://piMVtxlx7qUl5BihwhDhWJd2Msj.zip) (37.2 KB)

author: ChaosFish
raw: The game first checks if there are vox files on C:\Games\LBA\LBA1\VOX\  (or whatever is your lba folder) and if it doesn't find it it checks for the cd.
cooked: The game first checks if there are vox files on C:\Games\LBA\LBA1\VOX\ (or whatever is your lba folder) and if it doesnt find it it checks for the cd.

author: Battler
raw:
Well... I met a guy on the ICQ, who is from South Korea, from Gwangju (or how is it spelled), and who started to make a Korean translation for LBA 2 :eek:, but he didn't know, where to find suitable programs. I sent him my WinHQR and N-SoftInd's LBA Text File Editor, and made him a font (which I'm going to publicly release tomorrow). There was only one problem: that for complete Korean, LBA 2 would have to be Unicode or at least DBCS version, so the translation will be in Hangul Jamo.
 
As evidence, I attached some screenshots, of what so far has been translated (3 in-game and 1 menu screenshots, tested on lbaguy3d's older version of LBA :-/). ;)
cooked:
Well I met a guy on the ICQ, who is from South Korea, from Gwangju (or how is it spelled), and who started to make a Korean translation for LBA 2 :eek:, but he didnt know, where to find suitable programs. I sent him my WinHQR and N-SoftInds LBA Text File Editor, and made him a font (which Im going to publicly release tomorrow). There was only one problem: that for complete Korean, LBA 2 would have to be Unicode or at least DBCS version, so the translation will be in Hangul Jamo.

As evidence, I attached some screenshots, of what so far has been translated (3 in-game and 1 menu screenshots, tested on lbaguy3ds older version of LBA :-/).

topic_title: Do you want edit your LBA2 saved games?
author: xesf
raw:
Did you check twice what you did for compressed files?? Are you using mode 1??

File formats can't be different from the autosave.



```
00:	byte	Savegame identify, 0xA4 (compressed) or 0x24 (not compressed)
01:     byte	Scenes Code (see further)	
02:             unused
03:             unused
04:             unused
05:     string  Title
                * = from the end of the title, (after the 0x00)

[b]If the identify flag its 0xA4 the next data is compressed in mode 1
You need to decompressed first after editing it.[/b]

\*00:	s16	Decompressed size
\*02:	        unused
\*03:		unused
\*04...*end:	Data to decompress

[b]After decompress or if the identify flag its 0x24 the next data is an 160x120 Raw Image.[/b]

\*00...*19199	Image data
		 + = after the 19200 image bytes
```

topic_title: Do you want edit your LBA2 saved games?
author: Battler
raw:
\- alexfont: Yes, I checked. And I also found the difference. After the inventory items, there are 300 bytes in CURRENT.LBA/AUTOSAVE.LBA, but 304 bytes in the general saved games.

Edit: The 4 bytes apparently should be between the images and the inventory.

Edit #2: The inventory part of your format specification is really a bit wrong. Everything after the Protection Spell is wrong.

Edit #3: This is the partial correct sequence for that:
```
? ? ? Z ? ? ? J C ? ? ? ? ? B D c

Z = Zlitos
J = Jet-Pack
C = Crystal
B = Blowtron
D = Dark Monk's key
c = Clovers
```
Now I'm still looking for where is the Wizard's Tunic.

Edit #4: Found out what I was doing wrong when loading the saved games - the decompressed saved games don't have the decompressed size, while I was incorrectly loading them, as if they had, hecen they all began 4 bytes after their real beginning. I fixed this now. ;)

cooked:
My latest findings about the LBA 2 saved games:
- Part of the car: The translator has to be turned off for the part of the car to appear, but the part of the car doesnt have to be off for the translator to appear;
- Itinerary token: Turn on Pearl of Incandescence, and turn on variable on offset 4EF2 (D + 4B00 + 3E5);
- Super Jet-Pack: Turn on Proto-Pack, and turn on variable on offset 4F38 (D + 4B00 + 42B);
- Blowtron: Turn on Blowgun, and turn on variable on offset 4FA6 (D + 4B00 + 499);
- Offset 4D0E (D + 4B00 + 201): The guards attack you (0x01) or not (0x00);
- Dark Monks key: Turn on Fracos Key Fragment, and turn on variable on offset 4FF6 (D + 4B00 + 4E9);
- Wizards Tunic: Turn on Ancestral Tunic, and turn on variable on offset 4EE8 (D + 4B00 + 3DB);
- Current weapon: 4D7F (specified which inventory object is the currently selected weapon, by inventory object index, counting from 0) (D + 4B00 + 272);
- Horn of Blue Triton life amount: 4F94 (D + 4B00 + 487), max.: 0x64 (100);
Life points: 5109 (D + 4B00 + 5FC), max.: 0xFF (255).

Of course, all of this will be implemented into the LBA 2 Saved Game Editor, which will be released tomorrow.

author: Battler
raw: \- Gobbles: HQR replacements are possible, but you would also need a new LBA 2/TO .EXE file for that, which we can't afford to, as we don't have the LBA 2/TO source code. Sorry.

author: Gobbles
raw: But is it possible in anyway? hacking the file? it must be possible, but just not easy to do. Or could you reconstruct the game?
cooked: But is it possible in anyway? hacking the file? it must be possible, but just not easy to do. Or could you reconstruct the game?

author: Battler
raw: \- Gobbles: hacking the .EXE file is not a good idea for such big things. I tried to hack the LBA 2 .EXE file, in order, to display texts RTL (right-to-left), but I didn't succeed, since for RTL, I needed extra lines to be put into the code, which can't be done by hacking the .EXE file, as you can't change its size, otherwise, it won't work. Sorry. But... it is possible. Just we have to modify the scenes, redirecting some points into isometric scenes, instead of 3-D ones (I think there is some code in the scenes, which decides, whether to load a 3-D environment, or an isometric one). So, we should think about this solution...
cooked: - Gobbles: hacking the .EXE file is not a good idea for such big things. author: Battler
raw:
\- alexfont:
 
 I meant this: in all LBA 2 scene files, at the beginning, there is (I think) a bit, that determines, whether the scene is isometric (LBA_BKG.HQR) or 3-D (*.ILE). If it's 3-D, then it also specifies the index of the island, which data it has to load (or multiple, if there are various option, like for Citadel Island and Celebration Island), and then, it specifies, which part of the island it has to load (again, with the index). Of course, with 3-D scenes, all other parts (of the same island) are also loaded, to display the whole 3-D island. We just have to know, how to hack the bits.
cooked:
- alexfont:

I meant this: in all LBA 2 scene files, at the beginning, there is (I think) a bit, that determines, whether the scene is isometric (LBA_BKG.HQR) or 3-D (*.ILE). Of course, with 3-D scenes, all other parts (of the same island) are also loaded, to display the whole 3-D island. We just have to know, how to hack the bits.

author: Battler
raw:
Yes, folks, now you can play it. I made the grid for them using Zink's LBArchitect, and the scene with the moved trunks with my LBA Scene Manager. The .ZIP file, with the appropriate .LUN (scene), and .GR2 (grid) files is attached in this post.
Also, for best effect (to remove the 'S' symbol on the top, as it wasn't present in that version, at least, before you open the door), replace the entries 191-194 in the LBA 2 SPRITES.HQR file with 4 1x1 sprites.
So, I think a dream has come true tonight.

I'm looking forward to hearing from you all.

Yours sincerely,
Joo de Cames (OBrasilo).

[sewers_prev.zip|attachment](upload://7kZtw7wYrItqehDykodXCCYfmnn.zip) (7.85 KB)
cooked:

Also, for best effect (to remove the S symbol on the top, as it wasnt present in that version, at least, before you open the door), replace the entries 191-194 in the LBA 2 SPRITES.HQR file with 4 1x1 sprites.
So, I think a dream has come true tonight.

Im looking forward to hearing from you all.

Yours sincerely,
Joo de Cames (OBrasilo).

author: xesf
raw:
This file doens't have just 3 things inside, actually it have 90.

(3 offsets indexs with 32 bits + 0x0198 bytes of empty data ) * 30

All data before are just index to all this things:

1. => Credits. 
2. => Shadding pallete image.
3....88. => 26 models and 60 animations.
89...90. => Unknown...


;)

All data before are just index to all this things:

  1. => Credits.
  2. => Shadding pallete image.
    388. => 26 models and 60 animations.
    8990. => Unknown

author: Battler
raw:
OK. But is there any character, which I can use for identifying, whether an item is a body or an anim?
 
Edit: BTW, I found out, what the first item is. It specified, how many item sets are init (1 item set = 198h null bytes and 12 offset bytes, starting from offset 3h on (the first item is this thing, and it's not an item)). So, if you want to add 1 item, you must add 1 item set with 198h null bytes, and 12 offset bytes, and set the last 8 bytes to null (0h). Got it?
cooked:
OK. But is there any character, which I can use for identifying, whether an item is a body or an anim?

author: Battler
raw:
[quote="alexfont"]
This file doens't have just 3 things inside, actually it have 90.

(3 offsets indexs with 32 bits + 0x0198 bytes of empty data ) * 30

All data before are just index to all this things:

1. => Credits. 
2. => Shadding pallete image.
3....88. => 26 models and 60 animations.
89...90. => Unknown...


;)
[/quote]
Correction:
0000h: Number of character (personage) blocks;

NOTE: Each character block is 0198h empty (char. 00h) bytes + 3 32-bit offsets in the end.

0004h: Offset of credits text;
0008h: Offset of shading palette image;

000Ch-(Offset of credits text - 4): Character blocks of 01A4h bytes = 0198h empty + 0Ch (12 * 32-bits = 12 * 4 bytes) of the offsets in the end.

The size of each item must be get by (offset of next item - offset of current item), except for the last time, where its size is (complete LBA2.HQR-entry file size - offset of current item).


In the credits text, the characters 2Ah 00h 00h are used for line break, followed by either -, + or > for colors. ;)
cooked:
alexfont:

This file doenst have just 3 things inside, actually it have 90.

(3 offsets indexs with 32 bits + 0x0198 bytes of empty data ) * 30

All data before are just index to all this things:

  1. => Credits.
  2. => Shadding pallete image.
    388. => 26 models and 60 animations.
    8990. => Unknown

Correction:
0000h: Number of character (personage) blocks;

NOTE: Each character block is 0198h empty (char. 00h) bytes + 3 32-bit offsets in the end.

0004h: Offset of credits text;
0008h: Offset of shading palette image;

000Ch-(Offset of credits text - 4): Character blocks of 01A4h bytes = 0198h empty + 0Ch (12 * 32-bits = 12 * 4 bytes) of the offsets in the end.

The size of each item must be get by (offset of next item - offset of current item), except for the last time, where its size is (complete LBA2.HQR-entry file size - offset of current item).

In the credits text, the characters 2Ah 00h 00h are used for line break, followed by either -, + or > for colors.

author: Golemboy
raw:
blafard, for now you cant edit lba2 in the way you want. things you can edit are now:
LBA1:
all maps, text + fonts, scene (actors and where they go, but not what they do), music, just about everything except for actor life scripts

LBA2:
rooms (not outside maps), actors (what they look like and where they move but again not what they do), music, text and fonts

in terms of switching around HQR entries, check the file information site. if it isnt listed there, you cant do it

author: Battler
raw:
\- Blafards61:
1. It's not throwing. It's turning. It's because the viewer does this: RUN ANIMATION -> RESET -> RUN ANIMATION, instead of RUN ANIMATION -> RUN ANIMATION.
2. Explain yourself better, please.
3. You will fly, but only ahead, and it will be uncontrollable. To control the flying, do this: replace all the Twinsen Walking animations with Twinsen with Proto-Pack animations. It's the only way.
cooked:
- Blafards61:

  1. Its not throwing. Its turning. Its because the viewer does this: RUN ANIMATION -> RESET -> RUN ANIMATION, instead of RUN ANIMATION -> RUN ANIMATION.
  2. Explain yourself better, please.
  3. You will fly, but only ahead, and it will be uncontrollable. To control the flying, do this: replace all the Twinsen Walking animations with Twinsen with Proto-Pack animations. Its the only way.

cooked: the lightning spell empties your magic no matter what level or amount of magic you have? making a trainer for this is no harder than making one for any other game, just make a little program monitoring the data in the memory at the adress where the magic amount is stored and when it notices it changes, just reset it to full again, thats how most trainers works, why should it be impossible in lba2?

author: Battler
raw:
OK. Here's the format for that kind of fonts.
 
Each character in the font, as you see it in a file, represents a line of a screen character. For example:
A is (in MS-DOS, 8x8 stanard EGA/VGA font):
On-screen:

```

\--------
\---#----
\--###---
\-##-##--
\-#####--
\-##-##--
\-##-##--
\--------
```

 
Where - is bit 0, and # is bit 1.
 
Now, consider this:

```
2^0 2^1 2^2 2^3 2^4 2^5 2^6 2^7
B8  B7  B6  B5  B4  B3  B2  B1
```

 
Thus:

```
NUM(10) = B1*1 + B2*2 + B3*4 + B4*8 + B5*16 + B6*32 + B7*64 + B8*128
```

 
And you get (for A in MS-DOS):

```
00h 10h 38h 6Ch 7Ch 6Ch 6Ch 00h
```

 
And you have to consider, that depending of size, the font data is divided in parts of either 8, 14, 16, or 19 (8x19 is a rare font size, and it's almost unused) bytes, depending on the font (8x8, 8x14, etc.), where the 8, 14, etc. means the character height. Now, in the LBA case, we have a 8x8 font, thus there are 8-bytes-per-character. Now, do this: get byte-by-byte in a loop, and convert them into binary data, which you store in an 2-D array (a string of ASCII codes in binary format) (for example: [b]var chardata: array[0..255][1..8] of string[8][/b] in Pascal, where [0..255] is the character, and [1..8] is the pixel line, it's the same, as [b]Dim charadata(0 To 255)(1 To 8) As String * 8 in VB, where string[8] or String * 8 means a string with a fixed width of 8 bytes), and everytime you reach 8 bytes, increase the character counter variable (which has to have an initial value of 0) by 1, and re-set the line counter variable to 0.
 
For vice-versa, just convert the ASCII codes of the font file characters into binary format, and then consider:

```
If bit = 0 then this pixel is empty (has background color).
If bit = 1 then this pixel is filled (has foreground color).
```

 
The background and foreground colors are specified in DOS screen data with one byte, which is after each character (NOTE: has nothing to do with the data in the font files),
which in hex has 2 digits: digit 1 = background color, and digit2 = foreground color, thus 1Fh would mean bright white (Fh) on blue (1h).
 
For more, contact me, either by PM, or by e-mail, or on my forum. ;)

\-##-##--
\--------

Where - is bit 0, and # is bit 1.

Now, consider this:

2^0 2^1 2^2 2^3 2^4 2^5 2^6 2^7
B8  B7  B6  B5  B4  B3  B2  B1

NUM(10) = B1*1 + B2*2 + B3*4 + B4*8 + B5*16 + B6*32 + B7*64 + B8*128

00h 10h 38h 6Ch 7Ch 6Ch 6Ch 00h

And you have to consider, that depending of size, the font data is divided in parts of either 8, 14, 16, or 19 (8x19 is a rare font size, and its almost unused) bytes, depending on the font (8x8, 8x14, etc.), where the 8, 14, etc. means the character height. Now, in the LBA case, we have a 8x8 font, thus there are 8-bytes-per-character. Now, do this: get byte-by-byte in a loop, and convert them into binary data, which you store in an 2-D array (a string of ASCII codes in binary format) (for example: var chardata: array[0255][18] of string[8] in Pascal, where [0255] is the character, and [18] is the pixel line, its the same, as [b]Dim charadata(0 To 255)(1 To 8) As String * 8 in VB, where string[8] or String * 8 means a string with a fixed width of 8 bytes), and everytime you reach 8 bytes, increase the character counter variable (which has to have an initial value of 0) by 1, and re-set the line counter variable to 0.

author: Battler
raw:
Here's a MS-DOS font editor for Windows, made by me.
 
MS-DOS Font Editor version 1.0.0 by OBrasilo
============================================
1. You don't need to open a text for preview to edit the font. Just edit it in the main dialog box.

2. It can open from and save to two types of font files: .FNT (also .F16, etc.), and from LBA 2/TO executables directly. The support for the Code Page Information (.CPI) files will be added in the future.

3. To edit the font, just click on the area of the font. Left Click = fill area (set current pixel bit to 1), Right Click = empty area (set current pixel bit to 0)*.

4. When you quit the program, if you have the preview dialog box open, close it first (or you can also close it after the program, which is not recommended).

\* Current pixel bit = bit of that square in the font file (square (double pixel) in the program , bit in the font file).
 
Get it here: [http://www.obrasilero.com/FontEdit.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/FontEdit.zip). :)
cooked:

  2. It can open from and save to two types of font files: .FNT (also .F16, etc.), and from LBA 2/TO executables directly. The support for the Code Page Information (.CPI) files will be added in the future.

  3. To edit the font, just click on the area of the font. Left Click = fill area (set current pixel bit to 1), Right Click = empty area (set current pixel bit to 0)*.

  4. When you quit the program, if you have the preview dialog box open, close it first (or you can also close it after the program, which is not recommended).

* Current pixel bit = bit of that square in the font file (square (double pixel) in the program , bit in the font file).

Get it here: http://www.obrasilero.com/FontEdit.zip.

author: Battler
raw:
[quote=""Illumina (the signature)""]
How do the chineses people write in China on computers anyway? :geek:
[/quote]
 
It's simple: for Chinese (both Traditional and Simiplified), Korean, and Japanese, a system, called IME, is used. In this way, you enter the character in an alphabetical script (either Chinese Bopomofo (for traditional; Chinese Latin Pinyin for simplified), Korean alphabetical script, or Latin for Japanse, and the system automatically converts it into the appropriate special characters (of which, you must choose the best one yourself).

=================================

Author: Joo de Cames (OBrasilo).

Purpose: Replaces the 8x8 MS-DOS font inside the LBA 2 executables with an outside 8x8 MS-DOS font file.

Usage: LBA2Replace "font_file_name" ["lba2_path"]

NOTE: The quotation marks (") are obbligatory.

author: Battler
raw:
[b]LBA 2 Font Replacer version 1.5.0[/b]
=================================
 
Author: Joo de Cames (OBrasilo).
 
Purpose: Replaces the 8x8 MS-DOS font inside the LBA 2 executables with an outside 8x8 MS-DOS font file.
 
Usage:
LBA2Replace "font_file_name" "lba2_path"
or
LBA2Replace "lba2_path"
or
LBA2Replace
or
LBA2Replace /A:"font_file_name" (to archive the font into the .EXE file)
 
 
NOTE: The quotation marks (") are obbligatory.
 
NOTE: If only one parameter is specified, then it's considered the LBA 2 path, and the font file is considered to be LBA2.FNT inside that folder.
 
NOTE: If no parameter is specified, then the LBA 2 path is the current folder, and the font file is the LBA2.FNT file inside the current folder.
 
NOTE: If no parameter is specified, and there is no LBA2.FNT file inside the current filder, then the LBA 2 path is the current folder, and the font is retrieved from within the LBA 2 Font Replacer's executable file.
 
NOTE: If only one parameter is specified, and it's preceded by a /A:, then the parameter specifies the font and that the program should archive it in the executable file.
Also, some bugs were corrected.
 
Example: LBA2Replace "C:\FONTS\CP862.FNT" "C:\Program Files\Activision\Twinsen's Odyssey"
 
BTW: Hebrew font (CP862.FNT), 8x8, included, so you can practise a bit, before using this program in an installation package.
 
Get it here: [http://www.obrasilero.com/LBA2Replace.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/LBA2Replace.zip).
 
 
So now you have one file less to put into your installation package, Fishos. Just archive it into the LBA 2 Font Replacer's executable file. Also, you forgot to reverse these string of the key config menu:
"Left ALT", "Left CTRL", "Left SHIFT", "Right ALT", "Right CTRL", "Right SHIFT".
Can I try to reverse them for you or will you do it (if you do it, then send me the modified LBT file for that, of couse). :)

author: Battler
raw: \- morshem: Well... it's not a matter of Brazilian or non-Brazilian version. Actually, the Brazilian version is the same as others, only it's dated Jun 23rd instead of Jul 3rd, so the executable files in it are not exactly the same as in the Jul 3rd version (ex. the common European version of LBA 2). The U.S. version is also the same, but dated Jun 16th, so the executable files are even older, but here's the deal. The overall sizes of the executable files in the Jun 16th release are different, than the Jul 3rd release, so I knew they had different offsets from the very beginning, while those in the Jun 16th release are the same as in the size as the Jul 3rd release, just the offsets are different, so I didn't realize the offsets are different, until I tried my video thingy (O_Video) on them. And now, there's a great possibility of someone in Israel even having a Jun 23rd European version of LBA 2, thus having problems with the Hebrew translation of it.
cooked: - morshem: Well its not a matter of Brazilian or non-Brazilian version. Actually, the Brazilian version is the same as others, only its dated Jun 23rd instead of Jul 3rd, so the executable files in it are not exactly the same as in the Jul 3rd version (ex. the common European version of LBA 2). The U.S. version is also the same, but dated Jun 16th, so the executable files are even older, but heres the deal. The overall sizes of the executable files in the Jun 16th release are different, than the Jul 3rd release, so I knew they had different offsets from the very beginning, while those in the Jun 16th release are the same as in the size as the Jul 3rd release, just the offsets are different, so I didnt realize the offsets are different, until I tried my video thingy (O_Video) on them. And now, theres a great possibility of someone in Israel even having a Jun 23rd European version of LBA 2, thus having problems with the Hebrew translation of it.

author: Battler
raw:
[b][u]IMPORTANT ANNOUMCENET!!!![/u][/b]
 
Me and alexfont are now working on a LBA 2 disassembly project. We're trying to dissasemble the LBA 2 executable file with IDA (Interacitve DisAssembler), which is an excellent disassembler.
 
[b]Now, you would ask, what the heck is the purpose of this?[/b]
Well... once disassembled the file, we might be able to find out the formats for more LBA files, and also, where some functions in the game are. Think! Maybe we will be able to hack the LBA 2 EXE file to work in RTL! Or to display Arabic letters correctly! And not to mention the life scripts in the scenes! Or many, many other things... Just think of the possibilities!
 
So, just stay turned and wait for the progress.
cooked:

Maybe we will be able to hack the LBA 2 EXE file to work in RTL! Or to display Arabic letters correctly! And not to mention the life scripts in the scenes! Or many, many other things Just think of the possibilities!

So, just stay turned and wait for the progress.

author: xesf
raw:
These are the things we need to do, to accomplish the project.

[b]Main things[/b]:
Plot 100% complete
Basic Script 100% complete (13 chapters + 1 Alternate)
Script of Events, Quest and Riddles [in progress...] (completed for the demo)
Rooms design [in progress...]
Images design [in progress...]
Dialogs implementation
Voice Casting 70% Complete (Lead Roles), 20% Complete (NPC's)
Character and Objects implementation
Sound Effects
Musics 70-80% complete
Concept Art [in progress...]
Story Code [testing some cutscenes...]


[b]Things to grab during the above items[/b]:
List of Objects. (28 max) [in progress...]
List of Game Flags. (inventory items used, quests to do) [according with events/riddle script]
List of Chapters. [in progress...] (2 chapters)
List of Magic Levels [done] (same as LBA1)
List of Locations [in progress...]
List of Holomap Locations [in progress...]
List of Disappearing Grids.
List of Dialogs and his indexs.
List of Character Names. [in progress...]
List of Characters Per Location. [in progress...]
List of Actions will each character have during the quest.
List of Character Definitions (life points, strenght, etc and which dialogs each only will use).
List of Character Animations
List of Sprites / Bricks
List of Midis
List of Samples


[b]In Plain English (08.08.07):[/b]
We've completed a number of new script chapters since the last update, and have sketched out a rough version of the plot to near game completion. Concept art is constant, and Luke has done a great job, especially with storyboarding. Similarly, we have some great renders from LG. The grids from Zink, Alex, Obras, and now Jesse too are very good, and Alex and Zink are continuing work on programs. We're much further than we were 6 months ago. The project is still on pace for a late 2007 release, and we should begin recording final voices sometime in the fall.

The script/plot are finally complete! We now know exactly what Hegesippe does, why he does it, and how it happens. Hoorah. Luke has also pounded out some great concept art that is allowing us to work on grids and character models very nicely. Currently, we are planning a Demo release sometime for late August/early September (a hard release date will be coming soon). Also, keep sending us your voice auditions, MBN'ers. We need as many people as possible to fill in the thousands of NPC's across Twinsun! :)

author: Battler
raw:
Yes, I promised it 18 months ago (when I mentioned it on my first site as "LBA 2 Island Height Map Viewer"), and here it is now.
   
   [b]LBA 2 Island Polygon Viewer version 0.01 Beta[/b]
   =================================================
   
   Current features:
   - Loading directly from .ILE files;
   - Viewing each part from 4 different angles.
   
   Things to do:
   - Viewing the whole island in one piece;
   - Viewing textures, clound and sea data, etc.;
   - Editing islands.
   
   
   How this program works
   ----------------------
 You load a LBA 2 .ILE file. The program will automatically draw the first area. You can change areas with the vertical scroll bar near the text box. You can change the angle of view with the horizontal scroll bar under the "Rotate:" label. You will see a polygon drawn in the picture box on the right of the window.
   
   
   Credits
   -------
   Me (OBrasilo): for starting the program and general coding.
   Krzysztof Adamczewski (kat15pl): for supporting me with this program.
   
   
   Here is the program: [http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView.zip).
   And the source code: [http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView-source.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView-source.zip).
  
  Edit: Oh, and BTW, here is a screenshot of the program, when viewing a part of Otringal:
  ![](http://www.obrasilero.com/ileview.jpg)
   
   Enjoy it. ;)

author: Battler
cooked:
LBA 2 Island Polygon Viewer version 0.02 Beta

New features:

  • Option to change the amount of lines/triangles per point;
  • Four different zooms;
  • Controls are now disabled until a file is open;
  • Whenever its needed to re-draw the polygon, the drawing procedure is now called (before, all instances had the drawing code);
  • Removed the grid;
  • Fixed program crash, when you press Cancel in the Load Island dialog box;
  • Exporting polygons to .BMP pictures.

Current features:

  • Loading directly from .ILE files;
  • Viewing each part from 4 different angles.

Things to do:

  • Displaying only what should be visible (currently (as well as in version 0.01), the parts that should be covered (because theyre behind our viewpoint) are still drawn);
  • Viewing the whole island in one piece;
  • Viewing textures, clound and sea data, etc.;
  • Editing islands.

How this program works

You load a LBA 2 .ILE file. The program will automatically draw the first area. You can change areas with the vertical scroll bar near the text box. You can change the angle of view with the horizontal scroll bar under the Rotate: label. You will see a polygon drawn in the picture box on the right of the window.

NOTE: When exporting to .BMP, if the polygon is zoomed to 2x default or 4x default, the whole polygon is exported, and not just the part you see in the PictureBox.

Credits

Me (OBrasilo): for starting the program and general coding.
Krzysztof Adamczewski (kat15pl): for supporting me with this program.

 Here is the program: [http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView.zip).
 And the source code: [http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView-source.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/ILEView-source.zip).

Edit: Oh, and BTW, here is a screenshot of this version of the program, when viewing a part of Desert Island:

author: Battler
raw:
\- Jasiek: You mean saving the height map as .BMP? OK, I'll try to do that feature in version 0.03. But there would be a problem - all heights are 16-bit and range from 0 to 32767.
 Now, if I convert them to 0-255 heights, it makes it possible to save them as greyscale BMP's, but they won't be as detailed. I can also save the height map as cyan gradients - that would be better, because I would use the first byte of the height as green value and second byte as blue value, and the red value would always be 0.
 But however, to solve the dilemma, I'll put both - 2 buttons: "Export height map as grayscale bitmap" and "Export height map as cyan gradient bitmap".
 Oh and BTW, check a post there in the LBA Scene Manager thread, it contains the link to a file which you need to download, if you want to run VB programs.
  
   - kat15pl: Stop asking me about that. I don't know how to put textures neither. :-/
   
   - Link: Thanks. I'll keep up the good work. ;)
cooked:
- Jasiek: You mean saving the height map as .BMP? OK, Ill try to do that feature in version 0.03. But there would be a problem - all heights are 16-bit and range from 0 to 32767.
Now, if I convert them to 0-255 heights, it makes it possible to save them as greyscale BMPs, but they wont be as detailed. I can also save the height map as cyan gradients - that would be better, because I would use the first byte of the height as green value and second byte as blue value, and the red value would always be 0.
But however, to solve the dilemma, Ill put both - 2 buttons: Export height map as grayscale bitmap and Export height map as cyan gradient bitmap.
Oh and BTW, check a post there in the LBA Scene Manager thread, it contains the link to a file which you need to download, if you want to run VB programs.

  • kat15pl: Stop asking me about that. I dont know how to put textures neither.

  • Link: Thanks. Ill keep up the good work.

author: Battler
raw:
\- StreGGy: Actually, the buildings, statues, etc. are not part of the island polygon. They're objects, stored in the .OBL files, and they're only put on top of the island polygon later during the game. So basically, with my program, you can (for now) only see the island itself, without the objects, buildings, etc. there.
 
 Oh, and BTW, OBL stands for OBject Library. ;)
cooked:
- StreGGy: Actually, the buildings, statues, etc. are not part of the island polygon. Theyre objects, stored in the .OBL files, and theyre only put on top of the island polygon later during the game. So basically, with my program, you can (for now) only see the island itself, without the objects, buildings, etc. there.

Oh, and BTW, OBL stands for OBject Library.

author: Battler
cooked:
Yes, with this thread, Im officially starting The LBA 2 Engine Project.

Goal? To reverse-engineer LBA 2 back to C/C++.

Heres whats already done:
- Main menu [Incomplete];
- Body models (thanks to yaz0r).

Anyway, heres the main menu part (incomplete for now):

void InitializeMainMenu();
// NOTE: Setting the X and Y coordinate of the buttons is not yet implemented.
int Idx;
string Text;
{
    Idx = 0;
    if (game.InGame == true)
    {
        Idx = 1;
    }
    //InText(Text Data Index (*2 = Text File, (*2)+1=Text Index Table), Text Index)
    Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Load game" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    Idx++;
    Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Save game" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    if (game.InGame == false)
    {
        Idx++;
    }
    Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Options" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    Idx++;
    Text = InText{Menu Text Index}, {"Quit" index});
    mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    if (game.InGame == true)
    {
        Idx = 0;
        Text = InText({Menu Text Index}, {"Return to game" index});
        mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption = Text;
    }
    if (game.InGame == true)
    {
        mainMenu.ItemsCount = 4;
    }
    else
    {
        mainMenu.ItemsCount = 6;
    }
}

void DrawMainMenu();
{
    DrawImage(LoadHQR("SCREEN.HQR", MenuBackgroundIndex));
    for (int i = 1; i <= mainMenu.ItemsCount; i++)
    {
        DrawMenuButton(mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].X, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Y);
        if (mainMenu.SelectedMenuItem = idx)
        {
            DrawMenuButtonHighlight(mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Caption, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].X, mainMenu.MenuItems[Idx].Y);
        }
    }
}

void LoadMainMenu();
{
    int ReadStr;

    InitializeMainMenu;
    mainMenu.SelectedMenuItem = 0;
    DrawMainMenu;
    while (mainMenu.MenuString != "\027")
        ReadStr = BackgroundCharRead;
        mainMenu.MenuString += ReadStr;
        // NOTE: The correct reading of the string has yet to be implemented,
        // but I unfortunately don't know, how to get the rightmost n characters
        // out of a string in C.
        if (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "LIFE")
        {
            twinsen.Life = maxLife;
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "MAGIC")
        {
            twinsen.Magic = maxMagic[twinsen.magicLevel];
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase =="GOLD")
        {
            // NOTE: This part can be reduced, what will be done later.
            if (game.Planet > 0)
            {
                if (twinsen.Zlitos < 999)
                {
                    if (twinsen.Zlitos < 950)
                    {
                        twinsen.Zlitos += 50;
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        twinsen.Zlitos = 999;
                    }
                }
            }
            else
            {
                if (twinsen.Kashes < 999)
                {
                    if (twinsen.Kashes < 950)
                    {
                        twinsen.Kashes += 50;
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        twinsen.Kashes = 999;
                    }
                }
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "PINGOUIN")
        {
            if (twinsen.InventoryItems[Pingouin].Count < 10)
            {
                twinsen.InventoryItems[Pingouin].Count += 5;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "CLOVER")
        {
            for (int i; i <= twinsen.BoxCount; i++)
            {
                twinsen.BoxFull[i] = true;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "BOX")
        {
            if (twinsen.BoxCount < 10)
            {
                twinsen.BoxCount++;
                for (int i; i <= twinsen.BoxCount; i++)
                {
                    twinsen.BoxFull[i] = true;
                }
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "FULL")
        {
            twinsen.Life = maxLife;
            twinsen.InventoryItems[TritonHorn].Life = maxHornLife;
            twinsen.Magic = maxMagic[twinsen.magicLevel];
            for (int i; i <= twinsen.BoxCount; i++)
            {
                twinsen.BoxFull[i] = true;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString.ToUppercase == "SPEED")
        {
            if (game.showCoordinates == false)
            {
                game.showCoordinates = true;
            }
            else
            {
                game.showCoordinates = false;
            }
            mainMenu.MenuString = "";
        }
        elseif (mainMenu.MenuString == "\027")
        {
            BackToGame;
        }
        elseif ((mainMenu.MenuString == "\013") || (mainMenu.MenuString     == " "))
        {
            MainMenuCommand(mainMenu.SelectedItem);
        }
        // Menu movement has yet to be implemented.
    }
}

I would, of course, need help from anyone who knows some C and/or C++.

Edit: Could some mod split this post from this thread, please?

author: Battler
cooked:
- alexfont: OK, thanks for the tip. I think Ill go from TwinEngine and just start modifying it from LBA 1 to LBA 2.

So first, I have to change some things in that menu:

  1. Add the cheat codes;
  2. Make the menu flexible, since in LBA 2, when you call the main menu from in-game, it has 6 buttons while when you start the executable file, it has 4 buttons; 5 buttons if theres a CURRENT.LBA file in the SAVE folder;
  3. Modify the borders to be rounder;
  4. Add the code to display the demo screenshots if the SCRSHOT.HQR is present and/or the #IFDEF DEMO is set;
  5. Make the CURRENT.LBA image be displayed when the CURRENT.LBA file is found in the SAVE folder;
  6. Modify the background image to be loaded from SCREEN.HQR instead of RESS.HQR as it is in LBA 1.

Anyway, Ill put into it the LBA 2 models code since I have it, thanks to yaz0r.

And yes, I agree with you. With just modifying TwinEngine from LBA 1 to LBA 2 we can have bigger compatibility between the two and also have the possibility to make some modification tutorials in the future, which will work with both.

Edit: I just submitted the registration for this project in SourceForge now, but I have to wait 2 days for it to be accepted (or possibly rejected) though

And in the end, thanks for splitting the thread.

author: Battler
raw:
\- Dan2552: You can already make an isometric LBA 2 outside - just convert the outside Brick Layout Libraries from LBA 1 to LBA 2, make some new bricks, layouts and libraries (mainly for Emerald Moon, Zeelich and Zeelich Undergas) and then make the grids using Zink's LBArchitect.

But yes, with this LBA 2 engine, it would be done much more easily than with LBArchitect + LBA Brick Layout Editor, since we will certainly learn many new things from it.
cooked:

But yes, with this LBA 2 engine, it would be done much more easily than with LBArchitect + LBA Brick Layout Editor, since we will certainly learn many new things from it.

Symbian is an Operating System for mobile phones. For example, Nokia use it in his last models. I'm now only trying for Nokia Serie60 phones because I have one (6630), anyway I didn't manage to get it to work yet because I need to change things in the project code including the mainSDL.c file that make possible run the game with the SDL interface. Anyway I already fix somethings, I just need time for it, thing that I don't have much this days :(.
But if I didn't manage to make it I'll ask help with some Symbian Guru's, it will be really great to have LBA on the mobile. I have now Monkey Island and the Day Of The Tentacle thought ScummVM and its great ;). Also the great Another World game, who doesn't remember this classic...

Hey Seb,
You can make the website but I think you shoudn't loose your time seting up a forum. I'll personaly not using it and for the bug tracking system we have SF, thats why we'll also have the project there.
For now the website only need to display only one page that shows we start planning it.

The project stills in the very beginning and I only did the SDL functions, HQR management and the only thing is showed are the first screens related with the right version of the game used.

About the ports: I'll not post the CVS till I manage to decode the LBA2 main menu (still figure out the things), and about the ports, I'll only release a win version because people who use linux could easly get the source and compile it. About the MAC, only if someone have one and can compile it for us.
Anyway this is really, but really early to talk about this stuffs. We'll not see a release for months because this isn't easy and need time to learn and do the things, thing I don't have much now-a-days.

Ok, PM if you want.

author: Battler
raw:
[b]LBA 2 Credits Manager version 1.0.0[/b]
===================================

Current features:
\- Viewing and editing the credits text;
\- Exporting and replacing item;
\- Saving with compression.

Things to do:
\- Making the compression optional;
\- Viewing the shading palette image;
\- Better looking credits text view/edit area with WYSIWYG features.


How this program works
\----------------------
You have to load either the LBA2.HQR file or an external version of its only item. The latter is recommended, due to Visual Basic's slow decompression speed. However, once the thing is loaded, you'll see a tree of 3 items: credits text, shading palette and character blocks. The last item can be expanded, it will spawn an indexed list of character blocks. Each of them can be expaned as well, to show one model and two anims.

The operations you can perform on these items are:
\- View/Edit: for the credits text only - makes you able to view and edit it;
\- Extract: extracts the selected item;
\- Replace: replaces the selected item with an external item of the same type.


Credits
\-------
Me (OBrasilo): for initial LBA2.HQR only item specification and programming.
Alexandre Fontoura (alexfont): for help on the LBA2.HQR only item contents.
 
 
 Links
 -----
 The program: [http://www.obrasilero.com/LBA2Edit.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/LBA2Edit.zip).
 The source code: [http://www.obrasilero.com/LBA2Edit-source.zip](http://www.obrasilero.com/LBA2Edit-source.zip).


Screenshot
\-----------
![](http://www.obrasilero.com/LBA2Edit.jpg)
 

 I hope you'll find the program useful. ;)
cooked:

author: Battler
raw:
You can download it from here: [LBA 2 Russian Speech Pack](http://zoneofgames.ru/index.php/gb/file/452).
File size: 64.41 MB.
NOTE: It just contains the .VOX'es, and not the VIDEO.HQR as well. To use it, just change your spoken language to "English" and then install this speech pack.

And the Russian texts are here: [LBA 2 Russian Texts](http://zoneofgames.ru/index.php/gb/file/207).
File size: 510.1 kB.

To download, just left-click on the link, and on that site, click on the big yellow image. You know, the one which says "SKACHAT'" = "DOWNLOAD".

NOTE: The site the links link to is completely legal, since it does NOT offer WareZ stuff.

Enjoy. ;)

author: xesf
raw:
Since the Russian language isn't a official LBA language (the LBA engine doesn't handle that), if I'm not wrong, the English version was replaced by the Russian one. Was the Russian version release as original? Is the original version you wanna give?
The thing you could do is, replace the TEXT.HQR file of the European release (I can send it to you if you want) when you install it and the game will appear in english. If you wanna the Voices, well it will be more difficult because the space, but I could get a space the way you could download it. ;)

Btw, welcome to the forum and hope you like your stay ;)
